audit_status.source_base: Greek Ephesians text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for alive from death, grace through trust, nations once far/near, one new humanity, peace, household/temple. Anti-Jewish and anti-body safeguards included.

verses.restored: And you, being dead in your trespasses and sins/misalignments,
in which you once walked according to the age of this world, according to the ruler of the authority of the air, of the spirit now working in the children of disobedience,
among whom we also all once conducted ourselves in the desires of our flesh, doing the wills of flesh and thoughts, and were by nature children of wrath, as also the rest.
But the Divine, being rich in mercy, because of his great love with which he loved us,
even when we were dead in trespasses, made us alive together with Messiah—by grace you have been saved/healed—
and raised us together and seated us together in the heavenlies in Messiah Jesus,
so that in the coming ages he might show the surpassing riches of his grace in kindness toward us in Messiah Jesus.
For by grace you have been saved/healed through trust; and this is not from yourselves, it is the gift of the Divine,
not from works, so that no one may boast.
For we are his workmanship, created in Messiah Jesus for good works, which the Divine prepared beforehand so that we might walk in them.
Therefore remember that formerly you, the nations in flesh, called uncircumcision by what is called circumcision in flesh made by hands,
that you were at that season without Messiah, alienated from the citizenship of Israel and strangers of the covenants of promise, having no hope and without Divine in the world.
But now in Messiah Jesus, you who once were far away became near in the blood of Messiah.
For he is our peace, the One making both one and tearing down the dividing wall of the fence, the enmity in his flesh,
making inactive the Law of commandments in decrees, so that he might create the two in himself into one new human, making peace,
and might reconcile both in one body to the Divine through the cross, killing the enmity in it.
And coming, he announced good news of peace to you far away and peace to those near,
because through him we both have access in one Spirit to the Father.
So then you are no longer strangers and sojourners, but fellow citizens with the holy ones and members of the household of the Divine,
having been built upon the foundation of the apostles and prophets, Messiah Jesus himself being cornerstone,
in whom the whole building, being joined together, grows into a holy temple in the Lord,
in whom you also are being built together into a dwelling place of the Divine in Spirit.

safety_note: Ephesians 2 must not be used as replacement theology. The nations are brought near to Israel's covenants and reconciled into one new humanity in Messiah; this is shared access and peace, not contempt for Israel or erasure of Jewish identity.
